assessment.title: COMP 469 Week 1: AIMA Chapters 1-2 Multiple Choice

material.mattext: According to AIMA Chapter 1, artificial intelligence is mainly concerned with:
Building machines that only copy human mistakes.
Understanding and building intelligent entities that act effectively and safely.
Replacing all computer science with robotics.
Writing programs that never use data.
Which two dimensions produce AIMA's four approaches to AI?
Fast versus slow, and hardware versus software.
Human versus rational, and thinking versus acting.
Symbolic versus numeric, and local versus global.
Search versus logic, and learning versus planning.
Which list correctly names the four AIMA approaches to AI?
Acting humanly, thinking humanly, thinking rationally, acting rationally.
Searching quickly, learning slowly, storing data, showing results.
Programming, compiling, testing, deploying.
Sensing, moving, cleaning, stopping.
The acting humanly approach is most closely associated with:
Proving every conclusion with formal logic.
Maximizing a mathematical utility function only.
Matching human behavior.
Avoiding natural language.
The Turing test asks whether:
A program can always find the shortest path.
A robot can clean a room with no sensors.
A computer can run without an operating system.
A human interrogator can distinguish a machine's written responses from a person's.
Which capability is needed for a system to communicate successfully in a human language?
Natural language processing.
Disk scheduling.
Packet routing.
Memory paging.
Which pair of capabilities is especially important for the total Turing test?
Spreadsheet formatting and file compression.
Computer vision and robotics.
Sorting and hashing.
Static typing and compiling.
The thinking humanly approach tries to:
Ignore human cognition completely.
Maximize score without any mental model.
Build systems that model how humans think.
Replace psychology with hardware design only.
Which set lists three ways researchers can study human thinking?
Sorting, searching, and hashing.
Introspection, psychological experiments, and brain imaging.
Compiling, linking, and debugging.
Cleaning, moving, and stopping.
Cognitive science combines AI models with:
Only operating system scheduling algorithms.
Only database indexing methods.
Only computer graphics.
Experimental methods from psychology and related fields.
The thinking rationally approach is most closely connected to:
Formal logic and correct reasoning.
Human imitation only.
Random movement.
Image compression.
Why is pure logic not enough for many real-world AI problems?
Logic cannot be written down.
Real-world tasks often involve uncertainty and incomplete information.
Logic only applies to vacuum cleaners.
Logical systems cannot store facts.
Probability is important in AI because it helps systems:
Avoid using data.
Remove all possible risks automatically.
Reason rigorously under uncertainty.
Guarantee perfect outcomes.
The acting rationally approach tries to build agents that:
Always behave exactly like humans.
Act to achieve the best outcome or best expected outcome.
Never use sensors.
Only memorize previous answers.
In Chapter 1, a rational agent is one that:
Has perfect knowledge of every future event.
Always imitates the average human.
Never updates its behavior.
Acts to achieve the best outcome, or best expected outcome under uncertainty.
What is the key difference between acting humanly and acting rationally?
Acting humanly focuses on matching human behavior; acting rationally focuses on best expected outcomes.
Acting humanly uses sensors; acting rationally cannot use sensors.
Acting humanly requires probability; acting rationally forbids probability.
Acting humanly is always safer than acting rationally.
Why does AIMA use the rational-agent approach as a central organizing idea?
It avoids the need to define tasks.
It proves all AI systems are already safe.
It provides a clear way to connect actions, environments, and performance.
It requires every AI system to be humanoid.
Knowledge representation is used to:
Randomize all agent actions.
Replace sensors with actuators.
Make all environments episodic.
Store information about the world in a usable form.
Automated reasoning is used to:
Prevent a program from using memory.
Draw conclusions and answer questions from available knowledge.
Convert all problems into images.
Remove the need for algorithms.
Machine learning is primarily used to:
Improve performance from data, experience, or patterns.
Guarantee that all actions are optimal.
Eliminate the need for performance measures.
Make a program act without input.
Why does AIMA describe AI as a universal field?
AI only studies one kind of robot.
AI only applies to games.
AI methods can apply to many intellectual tasks.
AI avoids reasoning, learning, and perception.
Which list contains disciplines that helped form the foundations of AI?
Typography, painting, cooking, and music theory only.
Marketing, accounting, athletics, and journalism only.
None; AI has no foundations outside programming.
Philosophy, mathematics, economics, neuroscience, psychology, computer engineering, control theory, and linguistics.
Which is a reasonable example of an AI benefit?
Making uncertainty impossible.
Better decision support, diagnosis, accessibility, scientific discovery, or safer automation.
Removing all need for human judgment.
Preventing all software bugs automatically.
Which is a reasonable AI risk or concern?
Bias, privacy loss, misuse, unsafe behavior, or optimizing the wrong objective.
The impossibility of storing data.
The disappearance of all uncertainty.
The inability of computers to calculate.
How does the Vacuum World lab connect to Chapter 1?
It proves that all AI must use humanoid robots.
It removes the need to discuss environments.
It gives a simple setting for studying rational action, performance measures, and agent outcomes.
It focuses only on operating system design.
In AIMA Chapter 2, an agent is:
Anything that perceives an environment through sensors and acts through actuators.
Any program that stores files.
Any system that has no environment.
Any object that cannot act.
A percept is:
The complete source code of the agent.
The agent's sensory input at a particular instant.
The performance measure itself.
The environment's final score only.
A percept sequence is:
Only the most recent action.
The list of possible actuators.
The complete history of everything the agent has perceived so far.
The final state of the environment only.
An agent function maps:
Actions to course grades.
Actuators to sensors only.
Environments to textbooks.
Percept sequences to actions.
What is the difference between an agent function and an agent program?
The agent function is the abstract mapping from percept sequences to actions; the agent program is the concrete implementation.
The agent function is Python only; the agent program is always handwritten math.
The agent function is the sensor; the agent program is the environment.
There is no difference in AIMA.
An agent's architecture is:
The final answer key for a quiz.
The complete history of all AI research.
The computing platform, sensors, and actuators on which the agent program runs.
The list of student names in a class.
A performance measure is:
The same thing as the agent's current percept.
The standard used to evaluate how successful the agent's behavior is.
A random action generator.
A rule that forbids learning.
PEAS stands for:
Program, Evaluation, Algorithm, Search.
Percepts, Energy, Actions, States.
Planning, Execution, Analysis, Simulation.
Performance measure, Environment, Actuators, Sensors.
In PEAS, the Performance measure describes:
How success or failure will be measured.
Only the programming language used.
Only the number of sensors.
The color of the visualization.
Which PEAS description best fits the Vacuum World lab?
Performance: write an essay; Environment: a spreadsheet; Actuators: keyboard only; Sensors: none.
Performance: sort numbers; Environment: a database; Actuators: SQL joins; Sensors: network traffic.
Performance: clean efficiently; Environment: grid with dirt, obstacles, and boundaries; Actuators: Suck, movement, NoOp; Sensors: current location and square status.
Performance: draw images; Environment: color palette; Actuators: paintbrush; Sensors: microphone only.
Rationality differs from omniscience because rationality:
Requires knowing every outcome in advance.
Uses available information to choose the best expected action, not perfect knowledge of the future.
Means the agent never makes a bad move.
Forbids uncertainty.
A rational action can still lead to a bad result because:
Rational agents are not allowed to use percepts.
Performance measures do not matter.
Rationality means acting randomly.
The agent may face uncertainty, incomplete information, or bad luck.
Autonomy in an intelligent agent means the agent:
Can rely on its own experience and learning rather than only built-in knowledge.
Has no sensors.
Never changes behavior.
Ignores the environment.
An environment is fully observable when:
The agent receives no percepts.
The performance measure is hidden from the instructor.
The agent's sensors provide all information relevant to choosing an action.
The world is always continuous.
The Lab 01 Vacuum World is usually partially observable because:
The agent always sees the entire grid perfectly.
The agent typically perceives only its current location and current square status.
The environment has no boundaries.
The agent has no actions.
Which statement best distinguishes deterministic and stochastic environments?
Deterministic environments require two agents; stochastic environments require one.
Deterministic environments are always continuous; stochastic environments are always discrete.
Deterministic environments have no states.
Deterministic outcomes are fixed by the current state and action; stochastic outcomes involve uncertainty.
Which statement best distinguishes episodic and sequential environments?
In episodic environments, each decision is independent; in sequential environments, current actions affect future states and choices.
Episodic environments always require robots; sequential environments never use actions.
Episodic environments are always unknown; sequential environments are always known.
Episodic environments cannot have percepts.
An environment is dynamic when:
It never changes during the agent's deliberation.
It contains only one state.
It can change while the agent is deciding what to do.
It has no performance measure.
An environment is discrete when:
Every value can vary over a continuous range.
It has distinct states, percepts, actions, or time steps.
It has no actions.
It changes randomly every time step.
A known environment is one where:
The agent sees the entire world at every moment.
The agent has no uncertainty about the future.
The environment must be episodic.
The agent knows the rules of the environment, including how actions affect states.
Which environment type is generally easiest for agent design?
Fully observable, single-agent, deterministic, episodic, static, discrete, and known.
Partially observable, multiagent, stochastic, sequential, dynamic, continuous, and unknown.
Unknown, dynamic, continuous, and adversarial only.
Random, invisible, changing, and unmeasurable.
Why are table-driven agents impractical for most real tasks?
They cannot be described mathematically.
They require no memory at all.
The table becomes enormous because possible percept sequences grow very quickly.
They always outperform every other agent design.
A simple reflex agent chooses actions mainly by:
Searching the entire future state space every time.
Applying condition-action rules to the current percept.
Reading the instructor's answer key.
Maximizing a learned neural network loss function only.
A model-based reflex agent improves on a simple reflex agent by:
Ignoring all previous percepts.
Removing all sensors.
Making the environment fully observable.
Keeping internal state about the world.
Which statement best describes AIMA's learning agent structure?
It includes a performance element, learning element, critic, and problem generator.
It includes only a table of fixed answers.
It cannot use feedback from experience.
It has sensors but no actuators.
